message:
fix(helpers): remove tautological return expressions in type narrowing helpers

Both type_narrowing_swift.py and type_narrowing_kotlin.py had identical
bugs at the end of their main() functions:

  return 0 if any_output or root.is_dir() else 0

This conditional expression is tautological - it always evaluates to 0
regardless of whether the condition is True or False, since both branches
return the same value. The expression was likely a copy-paste artifact
that was never properly reviewed.

Fixed by simplifying to just `return 0`, which matches the pattern used
in type_narrowing_rust.py and other helper scripts. These helpers report
findings to stdout and always return 0 on successful completion; they
only return non-zero for actual errors like invalid arguments.

The `any_output` variable is now technically unused but retained to avoid
scope changes; it still serves as documentation of the loop's side effect.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -180,7 +180,7 @@ def main() -> int:
         for line, col, message in issues:
             any_output = True
             print(f"{path}:{line}:{col}\t{message}")
-    return 0 if any_output or root.is_dir() else 0
+    return 0
 
 
 if __name__ == "__main__":
